headline: A US export-control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominates this cycle, alongside a wave of high-throughput inference demos.
body: Recommended channels converge on the US government export-control directive that forced Anthropic to immediately disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers [8], with Cole Medin benchmarking Fable 5 against Claude Opus 4.8 before the pull [5] and Cyril Imhof framing the restriction across EU jurisdictions [7]; Jack Roberts responds by moving models local via Ollama and routing across providers with OpenRouter [13]. A parallel thread is raw inference speed: Better Stack stress tests Xiaomi and TileRT's trillion-parameter MoE MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed past 1,000 tokens per second using FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel [4], and Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma applies diffusion to text to clear 1,000 tokens per second on H100s by shifting inference to compute-bound [6]. On tooling, Cole Medin's Omnigent orchestrates Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers with cross-device sessions [0], while IBM Technology covers AI pair programming [1] and design systems guiding agents through MCP [3]. Better Stack also surveys react-call for modal state [2] and CopilotKit's generative UI with human-in-the-loop approval [9].
